reasoning: The FRED statistical trend analysis establishes a solid P(increase) of ~0.30 for the near-term based on recent negative momentum over the last month and a negative historical seasonal expectation. We use this as our primary probability anchor, setting the immediate 1-week forecast to 0.35. While forex market analysts predict an appreciation of the Euro towards 1.20 by the end of 2026 (based on expectations of Federal Reserve rate cuts relative to the European Central Bank), exchange rates fundamentally behave close to a random walk in the medium-to-long term. To balance the primary statistical anchor with macro expectations, and explicitly following the rule that uncertainty must increase with the forecast horizon, probabilities are monotonically regressed from the 0.35 anchor towards a pure 0.50 random walk baseline for the most distant dates matching the 10-year horizon.
